layman_headline: Ethereum looks oversold and is bouncing hard — time to catch the fear discount.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode, which historically has been a good time to buy quality assets. Ethereum has jumped 11% in the past week after being beaten down over 60% from its all-time high. Bitcoin just hit a 3-week high on surprise low inflation data, and ETH tends to follow BTC with higher beta. The RSI indicator shows ETH is still near oversold territory even after the bounce, suggesting there's more room to run.
layman_action: Consider buying Ethereum around $1,880 with a safety net sell order at $1,740. If it reaches $2,150, take profits. This is a calculated bet on the fear lifting — don't risk more than you can afford to lose.
expert_reasoning: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold — a signal with 80% historical win rate in our tracking. The Extreme Fear index at 25 with an improving trend is another reliable signal (71% win rate). The Bullish Marubozu candle pattern shows pure buying conviction. While ETH is below all major MAs (bearish structure), the 11% weekly surge and BTC's inflation-driven breakout to $65.5K provide a macro tailwind. The risk/reward from current levels to $2,150 target vs $1,740 stop is approximately 1.6:1, acceptable given the confluence of oversold technicals and improving sentiment. South Korea classifying crypto as national assets adds a medium-term regulatory tailwind.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.3% means capital is flowing to Bitcoin over alts — ETH could underperform even if BTC rallies
ETH is still below all major moving averages in a bearish structure — the bounce could be a dead cat bounce before further decline
Ongoing DeFi exploit wave (Ostium $18M hack) could dampen confidence in Ethereum ecosystem protocols
watch_list: BTC price action around $65K-$66K — a rejection here could drag ETH back down
White House crypto bill meeting with senators — positive outcome could boost entire market
ETH ability to reclaim and hold above SMA20 — would confirm trend reversal vs dead cat bounce
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (25) but improving from 22 yesterday, with BTC hitting a 3-week high on low inflation data. High BTC dominance (56.3%) suggests capital prefers safety, but ETH's outsized 11% weekly move hints at a rotation starting.
context_banner: Extreme Fear with improving momentum and a macro catalyst — classic swing trade setup on oversold Ethereum.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal at 80% win rate — suggesting a high-probability bounce entry.
Extreme Fear at 25 but improving from 22 yesterday — historically a 71% reliable contrarian buy signal when trend is turning.
Bitcoin hit $65.5K on surprise low US inflation data — a macro catalyst lifting the entire market, with ETH showing higher beta at +11% weekly vs BTC's +5.7%.
ETH derivatives reset signals fresh positioning and potential for new directional moves, reducing overhang from old leveraged positions.
South Korea classifying crypto as national assets under modified law is a significant medium-term regulatory tailwind for major assets like ETH.
